text.title: Claude Best Practices Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules forming the production policy pack: tier-aware model selection, full-date pinning, explicit max_tokens, cache layout, retry-after parsing + shared bucket, batch eligibility.

rule.statement: Use Haiku for routing/triage/classification, Sonnet for generation/analysis, Opus only for complex reasoning/architecture; never use Opus in a high-throughput inner loop.
rule.rationale: Opus rate limits are tighter than Sonnet; a pipeline that works with Sonnet will 429 immediately with Opus.

rule.statement: Pin model ids with full-date suffixes in every production config AND log `response.model` (the model actually used) on every call, not just the model requested.
rule.rationale: Aliases can resolve to a different snapshot than requested; without `response.model` logging, fallback behaviour is invisible.

rule.statement: Always set `max_tokens` explicitly on every call; treat `stop_reason=="max_tokens"` on a structured-output call as INVALID and retry with higher max_tokens (do not parse a truncated payload).
rule.rationale: Default `max_tokens` is low; silent truncation produces invalid JSON that downstream parsers handle wrong.

rule.statement: Place stable content at the top of the system prompt with `cache_control:{"type":"ephemeral"}`; put dynamic instructions last; target `cache_read_input_tokens / input_tokens > 0.70`.
rule.rationale: Cache uses prefix matching; dynamic content before the cached block busts the cache; below ~70% hit ratio the 25% write surcharge erodes savings.

rule.statement: Parse the `retry-after` header from `RateLimitError` instead of guessing the backoff; in multi-worker orchestrators use a SHARED token bucket, not per-process backoff.
rule.rationale: Per-process backoff does not account for other workers consuming quota; the API supplies the exact wait time.

rule.statement: Use Batch API for any workload that tolerates 24h SLA — 50% discount on input + output with no other code changes.
rule.rationale: Batch API halves cost for offline workloads; no reason to skip when latency allows.
